stub: She is a Labour MP who comes from a white middle class background.
constituency: She is from a constituency that is not your constituency.
personalization: She tends to vote in accordance with her own beliefs, even when that conflicts with the position of her party.
substantive: She opposes the construction of thousands of new onshore wind turbines.
justification: In political debates, she regularly uses arguments that appeal to people like you (e.g. of your class and educational background).
responsiveness: Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, she said: “I did not go into politics to win elections, but to make this country a better place.”
text: She is a Labour MP who comes from a white middle class background. She is from a constituency that is not your constituency. She tends to vote in accordance with her own beliefs, even when that conflicts with the position of her party. She opposes the construction of thousands of new onshore wind turbines. In political debates, she regularly uses arguments that appeal to people like you (e.g. of your class and educational background). Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, she said: “I did not go into politics to win elections, but to make this country a better place.”
